description:
## Context

The visual viewer's Tier 1 (Mermaid, `workflow-design-visualize`, T-016) ships a static `<name>.diagram.md` with per-step and per-subagent drill-down tables. On a large blueprint the static document does not scale to navigation: there is no way to click a node for its detail alone, filter to agentic or gated steps, or follow a subagent reference. Tier 2 (the interactive browser viewer) was gated by `2026-05-30-workflow-design-advanced-tooling-spec.md` §3.4 until Tier 1 proved insufficient.

The user opened the Tier 2 gate on 2026-06-08 (a recorded decision per §4.4). `zhenya-vlasov/idea-finder` (MIT) is a working reference implementation of the parse-to-data-to-interactive-view pipeline this needs, which de-risks the build; we borrow its interaction patterns, not its code.

## Desired behavior

Add a self-contained interactive HTML viewer as a second render target of `workflow-design-visualize`. Phase A (this ticket) is read-only navigation; in-browser editing (Phase B) and execution-driving (Phase C) are deferred and gated. Full contract: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-08-workflow-design-interactive-viewer-spec.md`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `skills/workflow-design-visualize/scripts/visualize_blueprint.py` gains a `--html` mode emitting a sibling `<name>.viewer.html`; default output stays the Mermaid `<name>.diagram.md`.
- The HTML is self-contained: data, JS, and CSS inlined; no network fetch, no server, no API key; opens by double-click.
- No timestamp or randomness, so output is byte-identical on regenerate.
- The viewer renders the flow, per-node detail panels (steps + subagents), the 12-dimension panel, and filters; encoding matches Tier 1 one-to-one (no drift, no fabricated step-to-subagent edges).
- The Mermaid and HTML targets share one parsed model from the existing `yaml` extraction.
- Offline `unittest` fixtures assert the generated data model and a golden HTML smoke check; same bad-input exit `2` as the validator and the Mermaid path.
- `skills/workflow-design-visualize/SKILL.md` documents the `--html` mode and that the artifact is generated, not hand-edited.
- `skill_lint.py` passes; `WORKFLOW_DESIGN_SPEC.md` §9 and the advanced-tooling spec §3 updated to record Tier 2 as open.
title: workflow-design interactive viewer (Tier 2)
